asset_name: Blizzard VFX — Light snow particle strip
shared_properties: Particle cells use a compact 16x16px frame size so the strip can feed many instances efficiently.
Core palette fields remain fixed at primary #111111, background #000000, and accent #FFFFFF.
The particle language favors thin white flakes and short diagonal drift readable over dark and bright surfaces.
differences: This base strip supports the early and mid-winter snow flow used before severe whiteout states arrive.
Flake density stays moderate so building silhouettes and queue UI remain readable beneath the effect.
The 8-frame loop creates a gentle left-to-right drift that suggests steady blizzard motion.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the winter survival loop, queue-memory mechanic, building roster, and 1920x1080 Bevy target.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the monochrome blizzard mood, Viking settlement silhouettes, and snow density targets.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_village_scene
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the settlement composition, building spacing, and whiteout layering over the frozen basin.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: TextureAtlas particle strip instantiated by a weather spawner that emits lightweight sprite flakes across the playfield.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load obstacles/art_000005.png during weather boot and reuse the atlas handle for repeated particle spawn batches.
runtime_notes: Spawn flakes in screen space or world space according to the current phase while keeping motion deterministic at 60Hz.
Use pooled entities so the particle budget stays stable during long winter rounds.
Layer the particles below the heavy whiteout mask and above the far background.

context.usage: Baseline snowfall effect used through standard winter pressure and tutorial rounds.

shape.silhouette: Small diagonal snow flecks with a bright leading edge and sparse tail marks.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 128x16px with 8 frames of 16x16px and 0px spacing.
Each frame remains readable over both black HUD edges and white snow ground without large opaque blobs.
The flake motion suggests left-to-right drift when frames advance at 12fps.
